features.title: Authentication (login)
features.description:
POST /api/auth/login — public endpoint for JWT authentication.

Endpoints:
- POST /api/auth/login

Permissions:
- Public (no JWT required)
- All user roles receive role-specific metadata in response

Single-agency flow: user belongs to one agency (or none for SUPER_ADMIN). Returns HTTP 200 with: token (JWT), type ("Bearer"), role, nom, prenom, agenceId, agenceNom, agenceLogo, personneId.

Multi-agency flow (future): user belongs to multiple agencies and no agenceId provided. Returns HTTP 200 with: token (null), agencies (array of {id, nom}). Client re-submits with chosen agenceId to complete authentication.

Business rules:
- Anti-enumeration: 401 error message does not reveal whether email exists or password is wrong (same generic message for both)
- BCrypt password verification
- SUPER_ADMIN: agenceId=null, agenceNom=null in response
- ADMIN_AGENCY/AGENT: agenceId, agenceNom, agenceLogo matching their agency
- CLIENT: agenceId, agenceNom matching their agency, personneId set
- Deactivated accounts (actif=false): must return 401 (cannot login)
- Unactivated accounts (pending activation, no password set): must return 401

Test accounts in V2 seed data:
- superadmin@horoazhon.fr / Admin / SUPER_ADMIN
- admin@horoazhon.fr / Admin / ADMIN_AGENCY (agency 1)
- agent@horoazhon.fr / Agent / AGENT (agency 1)
- client@horoazhon.fr / Client / CLIENT (agency 1, personne 14)
- admin@immosud.fr / Admin / ADMIN_AGENCY (agency 2)
- agent@immosud.fr / Agent / AGENT (agency 2)

Edge cases:
- Empty body returns 400
- Missing email or password returns 400
- Non-existent email returns 401 (same message as wrong password)
- Login with deactivated account (actif=false): 401
- Login with unactivated account (pending activation): 401

features.title: Current user profile
features.description:
GET /api/auth/me — returns the authenticated user's information.

Endpoints:
- GET /api/auth/me

Permissions:
- Any authenticated user: 200
- No token: 401
- Invalid/expired token: 401

Response: { id, email, nom, prenom, role, agenceId, agenceNom, agenceLogo, personneId }

Business rules:
- Returns identity from JWT claims + database lookup
- Token must be valid and not expired
- No role restriction — any logged-in user can call this
- SUPER_ADMIN response: agenceId=null, agenceNom=null

Edge cases:
- Expired JWT: returns 401 with clear error message
- Malformed Authorization header (e.g., 'Bearer invalid', 'Basic xxx'): returns 401
- No Authorization header at all: returns 401
- Garbage token string: returns 401

features.title: Account activation and client invitation
features.description:
Activation token verification, account activation with password, and client invitation.

Endpoints:
- GET /api/auth/activation-status?token=xxx (public)
- POST /api/auth/activate (public)
- POST /api/auth/invite-client (restricted)

Permissions:
- activation-status: Public
- activate: Public
- invite-client: ADMIN_AGENCY and SUPER_ADMIN only. Other roles: 403. Unauthenticated: 401.
- SECURITY NOTE: invite-client is under /api/auth/** (permitAll in SecurityConfig) — role check happens manually in controller (securityUtils.isAuthenticated() + role check), not via @PreAuthorize. Verify AGENT and CLIENT cannot call it despite the URL being under permitAll.

Activation flow:
- GET activation-status returns {valid: true/false}
- POST activate accepts {token, password}. Hashes password with BCrypt, invalidates token (single-use). Returns 200.
- Expired or used token: valid=false
- Token expiry: 7 days

Invitation flow:
- POST invite-client accepts {personneId, email, agenceId (required for SUPER_ADMIN)}.
- SUPER_ADMIN must provide agenceId; ADMIN_AGENCY auto-uses own agency.
- Creates CLIENT account with activation token, sends activation email.
- Returns {message: "Invitation envoyee", activationUrl: "..."}.

Business rules:
- Tokens are UUID, single-use, 7-day expiry
- Password hashed with BCrypt on activation
- Duplicate email on invite returns 400
- Activating with already-used token: returns error (400)

Edge cases:
- Already-activated token returns valid=false on status check
- invite-client without agenceId when caller is SUPER_ADMIN: returns 400
- invite-client with duplicate email: returns 400
- AGENT calling invite-client: must return 403 (despite permitAll URL)
- CLIENT calling invite-client: must return 403

features.title: Password reset flow
features.description:
Forgot password request, token validation, and password reset.

Endpoints:
- POST /api/auth/forgot-password (public)
- GET /api/auth/reset-status?token=xxx (public)
- POST /api/auth/reset-password (public)

Permissions:
- All three endpoints are public (no JWT required)

Flow:
- POST forgot-password accepts {email}. Always returns 200 regardless of email existence (anti-enumeration). If email exists, stores UUID token in Compte.token_reset with 1-hour expiration.
- GET reset-status returns {valid: true/false}.
- POST reset-password accepts {token, password}. Hashes new password with BCrypt, invalidates token (single-use). Returns 200. Invalid/expired token returns 400.

Business rules:
- Anti-enumeration: forgot-password always returns 200 (critical security requirement)
- Token: UUID, 1-hour expiry, single-use
- Password hashed with BCrypt
- Multiple forgot-password requests for same email: each request generates a new token and invalidates the previous one. Only the last link works.

Edge cases:
- Nonexistent email on forgot-password: still returns 200 (anti-enumeration)
- Expired token (>1 hour): reset-status returns valid=false
- Reused token (already consumed): reset-password returns 400
- Multiple forgot requests: only latest token is valid, previous tokens invalidated
- Empty email field: returns 400

features.title: Authenticated password change
features.description:
Change current user's password without a reset token.

Endpoints:
- POST /api/auth/change-password

Permissions:
- Any authenticated user: 200
- Unauthenticated: 401 (manual check in controller via securityUtils.isAuthenticated())
- Note: endpoint is under /api/auth/** (permitAll in SecurityConfig), so auth check is manual in controller, not via Spring Security filter

Request: {currentPassword, newPassword}

Response (200): {message: "Mot de passe modifie avec succes."}
Error (400): validation errors from @Valid (currentPassword @NotBlank, newPassword @NotBlank @Size(min=6, max=128))

Business rules:
- Must verify current password via BCrypt before allowing change
- New password hashed with BCrypt and saved to Compte entity
- If current password is incorrect: 400
- If new password does not meet minimum requirements (6 characters): 400
- ChangePasswordRequest DTO has @NotBlank on both fields and @Size(min=6, max=128) on newPassword

Edge cases:
- Same password as current: allow (no error, just re-hash)
- Empty currentPassword: 400 (validation)
- Empty newPassword: 400 (validation)
- newPassword shorter than 6 chars: 400 (validation)

features.title: Property listing and detail
features.description:
Public read endpoints for property search and detail.

Endpoints:
- GET /api/biens (public, paginated)
- GET /api/biens/{id} (public)
- GET /api/biens/{bienId}/contrats (authenticated — returns List<ContratDTO> for the property)

Permissions:
- GET list and GET detail: public (no JWT required)
- GET contrats: requires authentication (SecurityConfig permitAll for GET /api/biens/* only matches single path segment). Any authenticated role (SUPER_ADMIN, ADMIN_AGENCY, AGENT, CLIENT) can access.

GET /api/biens:
- Basic params: search (string, filters city/address/postal/description), type (string), forSale (boolean), forRent (boolean), prixMin/prixMax (BigDecimal), page (default 0), size (default 10)
- Single-value params: caracId (Long), caracMin (Integer), lieuId (Long), maxMinutes (Integer), locomotion (String)
- Multi-value params via allParams map: caracMin_{caracId} (min value filter), lieuMax_{lieuId} (max travel minutes), lieuLoco_{lieuId} (locomotion type)
- All filters combine with AND logic. Dynamic JPQL query engine in BienService.
- Response: Page<BienDTO> with { content[], totalElements, totalPages, number }
- Each BienDTO: { id, type, rue, ville, codePostal, superficie, ecoScore, description, dateCreation, availableForSale, availableForRent, salePrice, monthlyRent, principalPhotoUrl, photoCount, photoUrls, agence }

GET /api/biens/{id}:
- Returns complete BienDetailDTO with nested: photos, caracteristiques, lieux, proprietaires, achat (or null), location (or null)
- Dual listing: both achat and location can be non-null
- Property with no photos: principalPhotoUrl=null (no error)
- Nonexistent ID: 404

Business rules:
- Dynamic JPQL: high complexity area for caracMin/lieuMax filters — multiple simultaneous filters must all apply with AND logic
- Speed ranking for proximity: A_PIED/MARCHE < VELO < TRANSPORT_PUBLIC < VOITURE

Edge cases:
- Empty search results: content=[], totalElements=0 (not 404)
- Negative page number: returns 400 (Spring Pageable validation rejects negative values)
- Very large page number beyond max: returns empty page (content=[], totalElements unchanged)
- No filters: returns all properties paginated
- Search with special characters (quotes, SQL-like strings): must not cause injection or error
- Multiple caracMin filters simultaneously: all must match (AND logic)
- Property with no photos: principalPhotoUrl is null, photoCount is 0
- forSale=true filter: only properties with an Achat listing

features.title: Property create, update, delete and sub-resources
features.description:
Authenticated write endpoints for property management and sub-resources.

Endpoints:
- POST /api/biens (create)
- PUT /api/biens/{id} (update)
- DELETE /api/biens/{id} (delete)
- POST /api/biens/{bienId}/caracteristiques?caracteristiqueId=X&valeur=Y&unite=Z
- DELETE /api/biens/{bienId}/caracteristiques/{caracteristiqueId}
- POST /api/biens/{bienId}/lieux?lieuId=X&minutes=Y&typeLocomotion=Z
- DELETE /api/biens/{bienId}/lieux/{lieuId}
- PUT /api/biens/{bienId}/proprietaire?personneId=X
- DELETE /api/biens/{bienId}/proprietaire
- POST /api/biens/{bienId}/photos?chemin=X&ordre=Y (NOT multipart — accepts URL string via query params)
- DELETE /api/biens/{bienId}/photos/{photoId}

Permissions:
- All write endpoints have @PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('ROLE_SUPER_ADMIN','ROLE_ADMIN_AGENCY','ROLE_AGENT')") at method level
- CLIENT: 403 on all write operations
- Unauthenticated: 403
- SUPER_ADMIN: can manage any property in any agency
- ADMIN_AGENCY/AGENT: can manage properties in their own agency only (agency scoping enforced in service layer)

Business rules:
- POST returns 201, PUT returns 200, DELETE returns 204
- Agency scoping: non-SUPER_ADMIN users restricted to their agency's properties
- Create: agenceId in CreateBienRequest. For SUPER_ADMIN, agenceId specifies the target agency. For other roles, agenceId is always auto-assigned from JWT — any submitted agenceId is silently ignored (safe behavior, property always ends up in caller's agency)
- Sub-resources: caracteristiques (valeur/unite query params), lieux (minutes/typeLocomotion query params), proprietaire (personneId query param only, no dateDebut), photo (chemin URL string + optional ordre integer, NOT multipart file upload)
- addPhoto returns 201 with PhotoDTO body; all other sub-resource POSTs return 201 with empty body

Edge cases:
- Update/delete nonexistent ID: 404
- Create with missing required fields: 400
- AGENT updating property from another agency: must return 403
- Deleting property with active contracts: must fail with descriptive error
- CLIENT role attempting any write operation: must return 403

features.title: Rental and sale listing operations
features.description:
CRUD for Location (rental) and Achat (sale) listings attached to properties.

Endpoints:
- GET /api/locations (list), GET /api/locations/{id} (detail)
- POST /api/locations (create), PUT /api/locations/{id} (update), DELETE /api/locations/{id} (delete)
- GET /api/achats (list), GET /api/achats/{id} (detail)
- POST /api/achats (create), PUT /api/achats/{id} (update), DELETE /api/achats/{id} (delete)

Permissions:
- Both controllers have class-level @PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('ROLE_SUPER_ADMIN','ROLE_ADMIN_AGENCY','ROLE_AGENT')")
- SUPER_ADMIN, ADMIN_AGENCY, AGENT: 200/201/204
- CLIENT: 403 on all endpoints
- Unauthenticated: 403

Location (rental): LocationDTO { id, caution, dateDispo, mensualite, dureeMois, bienId, bienType, bienRue, bienVille }
- POST body: {bienId, caution, dateDispo, mensualite, dureeMois}

Achat (sale): AchatDTO { id, prix, dateDispo, bienId, bienType, bienRue, bienVille }
- POST body: {bienId, prix, dateDispo}

Business rules:
- UNIQUE constraint on bien_id for both tables — one rental listing and one sale listing per property max
- Duplicate listing attempt returns 409
- A property can have both a location AND an achat (dual listing)
- POST returns 201, PUT returns 200, DELETE returns 204

Edge cases:
- Second Location on same bien: 409 (UNIQUE violation)
- Second Achat on same bien: 409 (UNIQUE violation)
- Delete nonexistent listing: 404
- Creating listing for nonexistent bienId: 404
- Creating listing on property from another agency (non-SUPER_ADMIN): should be blocked

features.title: Agency operations
features.description:
Full agency management with strict role-based access control.

Endpoints:
- GET /api/agences (public, returns array)
- GET /api/agences/{id} (public)
- GET /api/agences/{id}/biens (public, paginated) — for public agency profile page
- POST /api/agences (SUPER_ADMIN only)
- PUT /api/agences/{id} (SUPER_ADMIN or own ADMIN_AGENCY)
- DELETE /api/agences/{id} (SUPER_ADMIN only)

Permissions (@PreAuthorize confirmed in code):
- GET list, GET detail: Public (no auth) — SecurityConfig permitAll for GET /api/agences/*
- GET biens: Public — SecurityConfig explicitly permits GET /api/agences/*/biens (fixed: was blocked by single-star pattern, now has explicit rule)
- POST: SUPER_ADMIN only. ADMIN_AGENCY: 403. AGENT: 403. CLIENT: 403. Unauthenticated: 403
- PUT: SUPER_ADMIN for any agency. ADMIN_AGENCY for own agency only (403 on other agencies). AGENT: 403. CLIENT: 403. Unauthenticated: 403
- DELETE: SUPER_ADMIN only. All others: 403. Unauthenticated: 403

Response: AgenceDTO { id, siret, nom, numeroTva, rue, ville, codePostal, telephone, email, description, logo }
List of biens: Page<BienDTO> with pagination (page default 0, size default 12)

Business rules:
- SIRET must be unique — duplicate SIRET on create should return 400 or 409
- ADMIN_AGENCY can only update their own agency (agenceId from JWT must match)
- Agency deletion should be blocked if agency has active contracts or active users

Edge cases:
- Nonexistent agency ID: 404
- GET biens for agency with no properties: empty page (not 404)
- GET biens unauthenticated: must return 200 with properties (was previously 403, now fixed)
- ADMIN_AGENCY PUT on another agency: 403
- Duplicate SIRET on create: 400 or 409
- Delete agency with active contracts: should fail with descriptive error
- GET /api/agences returns at least 2 agencies from seed data

features.title: Contract listing and detail
features.description:
Read endpoints for contracts restricted to agency staff roles.

Endpoints:
- GET /api/contrats (paginated)
- GET /api/contrats/{id}

Permissions:
- Class-level @PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('ROLE_SUPER_ADMIN','ROLE_ADMIN_AGENCY','ROLE_AGENT')")
- SUPER_ADMIN: sees all contracts across all agencies
- ADMIN_AGENCY/AGENT: see only contracts for biens in their agency (agency isolation in service layer)
- CLIENT: 403 (excluded by class-level @PreAuthorize)
- Unauthenticated: 403

GET /api/contrats:
- Pagination: page (default 0), size (default 10), sorted by dateCreation DESC
- Response: Page<ContratDTO> { content[], totalElements, totalPages }
- ContratDTO: { id, dateCreation, dateModification, statut, type, bien (nested BienDTO), hasSignedDocument, cosigners[] }
- Statut values: EN_COURS, SIGNE, ANNULE, TERMINE
- Type values: LOCATION, ACHAT

GET /api/contrats/{id}:
- Response: ContratDetailDTO extending ContratDTO with: location (LocationDTO or null), achat (AchatDTO or null), siblingContratCount (int)
- CosignerDTO: { personneId, nom, prenom, typeSignataire, dateSignature }
- typeSignataire values: BUYER, SELLER, RENTER, OWNER
- Nonexistent ID: 404

Business rules:
- Agency isolation enforced in service layer — critical security requirement
- siblingContratCount = number of other EN_COURS contracts on the same offer (must be accurate)
- Every contract must have at least 2 cosigners

Edge cases:
- ADMIN_AGENCY requesting contract from another agency: 403 or empty/filtered result
- AGENT requesting contract from another agency: same behavior
- Nonexistent ID: 404
- siblingContratCount accuracy: verify it correctly counts only EN_COURS siblings

features.title: Contract creation
features.description:
Create a new contract with cosigners linked to a rental or sale listing.

Endpoints:
- POST /api/contrats

Permissions:
- Class-level @PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('ROLE_SUPER_ADMIN','ROLE_ADMIN_AGENCY','ROLE_AGENT')")
- SUPER_ADMIN, ADMIN_AGENCY, AGENT: 201
- CLIENT: 403 (excluded by class-level @PreAuthorize)
- Unauthenticated: 403
- Agency scoping: non-SUPER_ADMIN should only create contracts for listings in their own agency

Request body: { locationId (Long, optional), achatId (Long, optional), cosigners: [{personneId, typeSignataire}] }

Business rules:
- Exactly one of locationId or achatId must be provided (mutually exclusive). Both or neither: 400.
- Cosigners array must have at least 2 entries (minimum 2, no maximum)
- Contract type auto-inferred: locationId -> LOCATION, achatId -> ACHAT
- Default statut: EN_COURS
- Returns 201 with created ContratDTO

Edge cases:
- Both locationId and achatId provided: 400
- Neither provided: 400
- Fewer than 2 cosigners: 400
- Invalid personneId in cosigners: 400 or 404
- Invalid locationId/achatId: 404
- Creating contract for listing in another agency (non-SUPER_ADMIN): should be blocked (403)

features.title: Contract workflow, documents, and expiration
features.description: Contract status transitions, PDF generation, signed document management, and automated expiration.

features.title: Person operations
features.description: All person management: listing, search, detail, sub-resources, and CRUD.

features.title: User account operations
features.description:
User account listing, detail, creation, deactivation, and reactivation.

Endpoints:
- GET /api/users (paginated)
- GET /api/users/{id} (detail)
- POST /api/users (create)
- DELETE /api/users/{id} (soft-delete)
- PUT /api/users/{id}/reactivate

Permissions:
- Class-level @PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('ROLE_SUPER_ADMIN','ROLE_ADMIN_AGENCY')")
- SUPER_ADMIN: all operations, sees all users across agencies
- ADMIN_AGENCY: manage users in own agency, sees only own agency users
- AGENT: 403 on all operations (excluded by class-level @PreAuthorize)
- CLIENT: 403 on all operations (excluded by class-level @PreAuthorize)
- Unauthenticated: 403

GET /api/users: paginated (page default 0, size default 20). Each item: { id, email, nom, prenom, role, actif, activated, dateCreation }

GET /api/users/{id}: returns { id, email, nom, prenom, role, actif, activated, dateCreation, personneId (if linked), dateNais (if linked) }. Non-SUPER_ADMIN can only view users from their own agency (403 otherwise).

POST /api/users: body { email, nom, prenom, role, dateNais (optional), agenceId (required for SUPER_ADMIN), activationBaseUrl (optional, triggers email) }. Creates Personne + Compte with activation token (UUID, 7-day expiry). Returns 201 with { id, email, nom, prenom, role, activationToken }.

DELETE: soft-delete (sets actif=false), does not remove from database. Returns 204. Cannot deactivate equal or higher role (403).
PUT reactivate: re-enables deactivated account. Returns 204. Cannot reactivate equal or higher role (403).

Business rules:
- Duplicate email: 400
- Role hierarchy: SUPER_ADMIN (3) > ADMIN_AGENCY (2) > AGENT (1) > CLIENT (0). Cannot create/deactivate/reactivate equal or higher role: 403
- SUPER_ADMIN must provide agenceId, ADMIN_AGENCY uses own agency
- Deactivation is reversible (soft-delete)
- Agency isolation: non-SUPER_ADMIN can only manage users from their own agency

Edge cases:
- Create with existing email: 400
- ADMIN_AGENCY creating ADMIN_AGENCY: 403 (equal role)
- ADMIN_AGENCY creating SUPER_ADMIN: 403 (higher role)
- AGENT attempting any user operation: 403
- Reactivate already-active user: 204 (idempotent)
- SUPER_ADMIN create without agenceId: 400

features.title: Reference data operations
features.description:
CRUD for Caracteristiques and Lieux reference data.

Endpoints:
- GET /api/caracteristiques (public)
- GET /api/caracteristiques/{id} (public)
- POST /api/caracteristiques (SUPER_ADMIN only)
- PUT /api/caracteristiques/{id} (SUPER_ADMIN only)
- DELETE /api/caracteristiques/{id} (SUPER_ADMIN only)
- GET /api/lieux (public)
- GET /api/lieux/{id} (public)
- POST /api/lieux (SUPER_ADMIN only)
- PUT /api/lieux/{id} (SUPER_ADMIN only)
- DELETE /api/lieux/{id} (SUPER_ADMIN only)

Permissions (@PreAuthorize confirmed in code):
- GET (list and detail): Public, no auth required
- POST/PUT/DELETE: SUPER_ADMIN only. ADMIN_AGENCY: 403. AGENT: 403. CLIENT: 403. Unauthenticated: 403

Response: { id, lib } sorted alphabetically (findAllByOrderByLibAsc)

Business rules:
- POST returns 201, PUT returns 200, DELETE returns 204
- Alphabetical sorting enforced in repository
- Deleting a characteristic/lieu in use by existing properties: should fail with descriptive error (referential integrity)

Edge cases:
- Non-SUPER_ADMIN write attempt: 403
- Delete nonexistent ID: 404
- Duplicate lib: no unique constraint in DB — verify behavior (allow? or reject?). Decision needed.
- Empty lib value on create: must return 400 (validation required)
- Delete characteristic in use by properties (Contenir table): must fail or handle gracefully
- Delete lieu in use by properties (Deplacer table): must fail or handle gracefully

features.title: Client dashboard API
features.description:
Dashboard endpoints restricted to CLIENT role.

Endpoints:
- GET /api/client/dashboard
- GET /api/client/contrats (paginated: page, size params)
- GET /api/client/biens (paginated: page, size params)

Permissions (@PreAuthorize("hasRole('CLIENT')") at class level):
- CLIENT only: 200
- SUPER_ADMIN: 403
- ADMIN_AGENCY: 403
- AGENT: 403
- Unauthenticated: 403

GET /api/client/dashboard: returns ClientDashboardDTO { personneId, nom, prenom, totalProperties, totalContracts, activeContracts, totalRevenue, monthlyRevenue, properties (List<BienDTO>), recentContracts (List<ContratDTO>), revenueByMonth (Map<String, BigDecimal>) }

GET /api/client/contrats: Page<ContratDTO> — contracts where client's personne is a cosigner
GET /api/client/biens: Page<BienDTO> — properties owned by client's personne

Test account: client@horoazhon.fr / Client / CLIENT role / agency 1 / personne 14 (Girard Julien) — exists in V2 seed data. This personne is a cosigner on contracts 1, 3, 7 (as BUYER/RENTER). Does NOT own any properties (no Posseder record for personne 14).

Business rules:
- All data scoped to the client's linked Personne entity
- Revenue calculated from signed contracts
- revenueByMonth is a map of month -> amount

Edge cases:
- CLIENT with no linked personne: throws IllegalStateException
- CLIENT with no contracts: totalContracts=0, empty arrays
- CLIENT with no owned properties: totalProperties=0
- Non-CLIENT role attempting access: 403

features.title: Contract restructure: Bien-direct FK with offer snapshots
features.description: Restructure the Contrat entity to reference Bien directly instead of Location/Achat.

features.title: Property soft-delete with archiving system
features.description:
Add soft-delete (archiving) for Bien entities. Properties with contract history cannot be hard-deleted — they must be archived instead, preserving all data for legal/audit purposes.

## Schema changes
- Bien: add actif (BIT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1)

## Hard delete (DELETE /api/biens/{id}) — updated behavior
- If Bien has zero contracts ever (no Contrat with this bien_id): hard DELETE allowed, cascades Location/Achat/Photos/Caracteristiques/Lieux/Posseder as before
- If Bien has any contracts (any status): 409 Conflict with message suggesting archive instead

## Archive endpoint (PUT /api/biens/{id}/archive)
- Guard: if any SIGNE contract exists on this Bien → 409 Conflict (active agreement must terminate first)
- Side-effects on success:
  1. Delete Location offer if exists (property off-market for rent)
  2. Delete Achat offer if exists (property off-market for sale)
  3. Auto-cancel all EN_COURS contracts for this Bien
  4. Set actif = false
- Response: 200 with updated BienDTO
- Auth: SUPER_ADMIN, ADMIN_AGENCY, AGENT (own agency)

## Unarchive endpoint (PUT /api/biens/{id}/unarchive)
- Sets actif = true
- No side-effects — agent must manually create new offers
- Response: 200 with updated BienDTO
- Auth: SUPER_ADMIN, ADMIN_AGENCY, AGENT (own agency)

## Visibility rules
- Public endpoints (GET /api/biens, GET /api/biens/{id}, GET /api/agences/{id}/biens): exclude archived (actif=false). Archived Bien returns 404 on public detail.
- Authenticated endpoints (GET /api/biens with auth): include archived by default, with optional ?actif=true|false filter
- BienDTO: add actif boolean field
- Contract detail: always resolves Bien regardless of actif status (contracts reference archived properties)
- Client dashboard: shows only active properties in property list, but contracts still display Bien info

## Filter changes (GET /api/biens)
- New optional parameter: actif (boolean)
- Public (no auth): forced to actif=true
- Authenticated: defaults to null (show all), can filter explicitly

## Auth: SUPER_ADMIN, ADMIN_AGENCY, AGENT (own agency) for archive/unarchive

## Migration: Changes folded into V1 (schema), not a separate migration
